steps.title: Separate the discovery
steps.content: They found something never before seen in the fossil record is a complete clause. The next words, evidence of cloning, explain what that discovery was, so they need punctuation that introduces an explanation.

steps.title: Avoid fragments and run-ons
steps.content: Choice A leaves Evidence of cloning in attached to the next sentence in a way that creates a fragment. Choice B uses a dash for the explanation but then runs into In clonal fragmentation without a clean sentence break. Choice C uses commas that do not clearly separate the explanation from the process sentence.

steps.title: Keep the process sentence clean
steps.content: The phrase evidence of cloning should not absorb the following process description. Once the discovery is identified, the explanation of clonal fragmentation needs to begin as its own sentence.

steps.title: Introduce then define
steps.content: A colon correctly introduces evidence of cloning as the thing never before seen in the fossil record. A period then starts a new sentence with In clonal fragmentation, which explains the asexual reproduction process. The answer is D.
